dimension_explanations.alignment: As a zakat-eligible organization, it offers a medium fit for Muslim donors seeking to support religious and cultural causes focused on social justice [1]. The organization's mission aligns with urgent human rights advocacy, though its track record is primarily assessed through financial filings [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a solid commitment to its mission by maintaining an 82.6% program expense ratio [2]. While it shows strong capacity-building efforts, the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data results in a medium impact score [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.6%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core initiatives
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.10 to raise every $1
Maintains a healthy 7.3 months of working capital to ensure operational stability

alignment.explanation: The organization is a strong fit for Muslim donors as it <cite id="13">accepts zakat</cite> and serves categories including <cite id="14">fuqara and masakin</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Clarifying the <cite id="15">zakat allocation policy</cite> and specific eligible use categories would further assist donor alignment.

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="12">document a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway for its advocacy work.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with a <cite id="2">82.6% program expense ratio</cite> and <cite id="3">resilient working capital</cite>.

rich_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support systemic social justice and advocacy for the American Muslim community through a high-growth, zakat-eligible institution.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through advocacy for the oppressed
Value high program expense ratios where nearly all funds reach the mission
Support the development of Muslim institutional capacity in the US
Prioritize systemic change over direct service delivery
giving_considerations: The organization focuses on capacity building rather than direct aid
Executive compensation is high relative to the small revenue base
Impact is measured through advocacy outcomes rather than individual beneficiary counts
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-volume direct humanitarian aid (e.g., food parcels) or those requiring detailed cost-per-person metrics.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes in advocacy but a lack of rigorous third-party evaluation or beneficiary cost tracking.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization creates impact by mobilizing the Muslim community to advocate for human rights, though the specific causal pathway is not formally documented.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible policy changes or community protections.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a revenue of less than $1M and a high growth rate, the organization has significant capacity to absorb more funding for expanded advocacy.
strategic_priorities: Systematic Muslim involvement in human rights

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an extremely high <cite id="2">82.6% program expense ratio</cite> and high revenue growth.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="2">82.6% of its total expenses</cite> toward its social justice and advocacy programs, significantly exceeding industry benchmarks.
CSJ has demonstrated consistent expansion with a <cite id="6">3-year revenue CAGR of 22.7%</cite>, growing from $554,308 in 2021 to over $834,000 in 2023.
The charity maintains an <cite id="7">independent board of 8 members</cite> and earned a <cite id="8">perfect leadership score of 100</cite> from Charity Navigator.

strengths_deep_dive: The ICNA Council for Social Justice demonstrates a high degree of financial discipline, particularly in its fundraising operations where it spends only <cite id="5">$0.09 to raise every $1</cite>. This efficiency allows the organization to maximize the impact of its <cite id="1">$834,440 in annual contributions</cite>. Furthermore, the organization has built a resilient financial cushion, maintaining <cite id="3">7.3 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, which provides stability for long-term advocacy campaigns. Its governance is characterized by transparency, with <cite id="9">100% independent board members</cite> overseeing the strategic direction of the council.
rich_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice (CSJ) serves as a dedicated advocacy arm for the American Muslim community, focusing on the rights of the poor and oppressed. The organization operates with a lean financial model, reporting <cite id="1">total revenue of $834,440</cite> in its most recent fiscal year. Its primary mission is to facilitate assertive Muslim involvement in social justice through systematic initiatives and human rights advocacy. Financial management appears highly efficient, with a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 82.6%</cite>, indicating that nearly all funds are directed toward its core social justice programming. The organization maintains strong fiscal health, evidenced by <cite id="3">7.3 months of working capital</cite> and an <cite id="4">overall score of 86.86.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. By focusing on capacity building and community empowerment, CSJ addresses systemic issues affecting marginalized groups while maintaining <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.10 per $1 raised</cite>.
